methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $1.8B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: 1X Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $10B

private.name: Dexterity
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.65B

private.name: Collaborative Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M+

private.name: Fairmat
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $350M

private.name: GrayMatter Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $300M+

data.narrative:
Bedrock Robotics, a Series B robotics and manufacturing company founded by Boris Sofman, presents a valuation exercise against a fragmented peer set. The comparable universe consists of five private-stage peers in the same sector—1X Technologies, Dexterity, Collaborative Robotics, Fairmat, and GrayMatter Robotics—alongside one public comparable, providing limited but directional reference points.

Two triangulation methods apply here. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to the company's last post-money valuation of $1.8B and applying a typical Series B-to-next-stage step-up multiplier of 1.9x, implies a valuation of $3.3B. Peer-median analysis across the five same-sector, same-stage comparables yields a median of $500M, with a range spanning $300M to $10.0B—a wide dispersion reflecting heterogeneity in robotics subsectors and capital intensity. The peer data reveals one outlier (1X Technologies at $10.0B) and tighter clustering in the $300M–$1.6B band (Dexterity, Collaborative Robotics, Fairmat, GrayMatter).

The triangulated analysis supports a median valuation of $1.9B, with a defensible range from $425M (low) to $3.8B (high). The current $1.75B valuation sits near the lower bound of this range. Key uncertainties include whether Bedrock's technology and market position warrant premium relative to the $500M peer median, and whether the Series B-stage multiplier methodology appropriately reflects post-money dynamics in a volatile, early-stage sector.
